question_text: Explain the difference between boiling and evaporation.
correct_answer: Boiling happens at a fixed temperature throughout the liquid and involves bubbles forming inside. Evaporation happens at the surface at any temperature below the boiling point.
explanation: Boiling is a rapid change of state that occurs when liquid reaches its boiling point and bubbles form throughout the liquid. Evaporation is a slower process where particles escape from the surface of a liquid at any temperature, and is why puddles dry up on cool days.
